perturbation.original_text: (b) TouchStar will invoice Reseller for payment of the Fee incurred within ten (10) days after the end of each billable month. Payment of the Fees shall be due and payable within ten (10) days of the date of the invoice. All or any portion of the Fees not paid when due shall bear interest at the rate of one and one-half (1.5%) per month, calculated from the date such payment is due until the date on which such payment is made, inclusive; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that if such interest rate exceeds the amount allowed by applicable law, then the interest rate shall be adjusted to reflect the maximum amount allowed by such applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) TouchStar will invoice Reseller for payment of the Fee incurred within ten (10) days after the end of each billable month. Payment of the Fees shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days of the date of the invoice. All or any portion of the Fees not paid when due shall bear interest at the rate of one and one-half (1.5%) per month, calculated from the date such payment is due until the date on which such payment is made, inclusive; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that if such interest rate exceeds the amount allowed by applicable law, then the interest rate shall be adjusted to reflect the maximum amount allowed by such applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the payment due date from ten (10) days to thirty (30) days. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 8.2(a) states that TouchStar may terminate the agreement if "Reseller fails to make any payment of Fees due to TouchStar under this Agreement and such failure remains unremedied for a period of ten (10) days."
perturbation.contradicted_text: TouchStar may terminate this Agreement upon the occurrence and continuation of any of the following events, with the understanding that, if no cure period specifically is stated with regard to an event, then no cure period for such event applies: (a) Reseller fails to make any payment of Fees due to TouchStar under this Agreement and such failure remains unremedied for a period of ten (10) days;
perturbation.justification: The modification alters the payment due date from ten (10) days to thirty (30) days in Section (b). Section (a) allows TouchStar to terminate the agreement if payment failure remains unremedied for ten (10) days. Therefore, changing the due date to 30 days creates a direct conflict, as TouchStar could theoretically terminate the agreement before the new due date arrives, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 TERM OF AGREEMENT. This Agreement shall continue in force for a term of twelve (12) months from the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier under the provisions of this Article 8 (the "Term"); PROVIDED that TouchStar shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time after the Effective Date upon not less than fifteen (15) days' prior written notice to Reseller. Prior to the end of the Term, each of TouchStar and Reseller may notify the other if it desires to negotiate a further agreement by written request received at least ninety (90) days in advance of the termination of this Agreement. If both parties desire to negotiate a further agreement, they may consider the terms of this Agreement in coming to an understanding. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to obligate either party to renew or extend the term of this Agreement. Renewals for additional terms, if any, shall not cause this Agreement to be construed as an agreement of indefinite duration.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 TERM OF AGREEMENT. This Agreement shall continue in force for a term of twelve (12) months from the Effective Date, unless terminated earlier under the provisions of this Article 8 (the "Term"); PROVIDED that TouchStar shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time after the Effective Date upon not less than sixty (60) days' prior written notice to Reseller. Prior to the end of the Term, each of TouchStar and Reseller may notify the other if it desires to negotiate a further agreement by written request received at least ninety (90) days in advance of the termination of this Agreement. If both parties desire to negotiate a further agreement, they may consider the terms of this Agreement in coming to an understanding. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to obligate either party to renew or extend the term of this Agreement. Renewals for additional terms, if any, shall not cause this Agreement to be construed as an agreement of indefinite duration.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the notice period for termination by TouchStar from fifteen (15) days to sixty (60) days. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 8.2(a) which refers to curing payment failures still uses the old fifteen (15) day notice period as that term is never updated across the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TouchStar may terminate this Agreement upon the occurrence and continuation of any of the following events, with the understanding that, if no cure period specifically is stated with regard to an event, then no cure period for such event applies: (a) Reseller fails to make any payment of Fees due to TouchStar under this Agreement and such failure remains unremedied for a period of ten (10) days;
perturbation.justification: The change to Section 8.1 increases the notice period for termination by TouchStar from 15 days to 60 days. This directly contradicts Section 8.2(a), which provides a ten (10) day cure period for payment failures, implicitly relying on the original 15-day notice period for termination. The increased notice period in 8.1 makes the timeline in 8.2(a) inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, as TouchStar may now be required to provide a 60-day notice period, conflicting with the shorter period implied in 8.2(a)'s cure period for payment failures. This is a clear inconsistency as per the definitions provided.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 CUSTOMER AGREEMENTS. (a) In the event that (i) TouchStar terminates this Agreement in accordance with any one or more of the provisions of Section 8.2 or (ii) Reseller elects not to enter into a new agreement with TouchStar pursuant to Section 8.1, all Customer Agreements with Customers shall be transferred by Reseller to TouchStar. (b) In the event that (i) TouchStar elects not to enter into a new agreement with Reseller pursuant to Section 8.2 or (ii) Reseller terminates this Agreement in accordance with the provisions of Section 8.3, Reseller shall retain all Customer Agreements with Customers and TouchStar shall continue to provide Support Services under such Customer Agreements for the remaining term of such Customer Agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 CUSTOMER AGREEMENTS. (a) In the event that (i) TouchStar terminates this Agreement in accordance with any one or more of the provisions of Section 8.2, all Customer Agreements with Customers shall be transferred by Reseller to TouchStar. (b) In the event that (i) TouchStar elects not to enter into a new agreement with Reseller pursuant to Section 8.2 or (ii) Reseller terminates this Agreement in accordance with the provisions of Section 8.3, Reseller shall retain all Customer Agreements with Customers and TouchStar shall continue to provide Support Services under such Customer Agreements for the remaining term of such Customer Agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The modification omits "or (ii) Reseller elects not to enter into a new agreement with TouchStar pursuant to Section 8.1" from 9.1(a). This creates an in-text contradiction because under Section 8.1, Reseller has an option to not renew, but Section 9.1(a) now fails to address the procedure in this scenario, while 9.1(b) does still address a scenario where TouchStar elected to renew the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 CUSTOMER AGREEMENTS. (a) In the event that (i) TouchStar terminates this Agreement in accordance with any one or more of the provisions of Section 8.2, all Customer Agreements with Customers shall be transferred by Reseller to TouchStar. (b) In the event that (i) TouchStar elects not to enter into a new agreement with Reseller pursuant to Section 8.2 or (ii) Reseller terminates this Agreement in accordance with the provisions of Section 8.3, Reseller shall retain all Customer Agreements with Customers and TouchStar shall continue to provide Support Services under such Customer Agreements for the remaining term of such Customer Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 9.1(a) accounted for scenarios where either TouchStar terminates the agreement (8.2) OR the Reseller chooses not to renew (8.1). The changed text removes the Reseller's non-renewal option from 9.1(a). This creates a direct conflict with the existing text of 9.1(b), which still addresses Reseller's non-renewal as a condition. Thus there is now an in-text contradiction.
